name: qrc-demo-instant__2026-05-17
description: Instant-mode counterpart to qrc-demo-range-max — 2-node DAG (instant query → threshold, no reduce node). Counter pushed at 8 req/s via push-metric.py; real Grafana transitioned Normal → Pending @ 2026-05-17T07:36:30Z → Firing @ 07:38:30Z (Pending+2m). Push then stopped; rule remained Alerting (fires, never resolves) — same scenario as the range fixture, validates that an instant rule produces the same lifecycle.

provenance.ruleCreatedVia: POST /api/v1/provisioning/alert-rules on pantrypulse.grafana.net at 2026-05-17T07:33:23Z; rule deleted after recording (DELETE /api/v1/provisioning/alert-rules/qrc-demo-instant)
provenance.samplesCapturedVia: Grafana Cloud Prometheus query_range API immediately after push ended. Counter retention preserves the run.
provenance.expectedCapturedVia: Captured from Grafana's /api/prometheus/grafana/api/v1/alerts endpoint while the rule was active: state=Alerting, activeAt=2026-05-17T07:36:30Z. Pending start = activeAt; Firing start = activeAt + spec.for (2m). Firing.until uses emit.ts's open-lifecycle extension (lastSample.t + evaluationInterval = 1779003638000 + 60000).
knownDivergencesNotAsserted: Tick wallclock alignment. Grafana evaluates at its scheduler cadence (independent of when samples started); alert-whatif anchors ticks at samples[0].t. The test allows ±60s tolerance on Pending/Firing entry times — Grafana's own ±60s natural jitter per trigger.interval=1m.
Resolution timing. This fixture captures 'fires, never resolves' (push stopped while still firing); no Resolved event is asserted.
